text: Tesla shares fell after the company warned that gross margins may remain under pressure next quarter despite stronger delivery volumes.
Apple reported better-than-expected services revenue and said installed base reached a new all-time high.
AMD beat revenue expectations, but management guided next-quarter revenue below consensus due to softer embedded demand.
JPMorgan said credit losses remain below historical averages and net interest income is tracking ahead of prior guidance.
The company cautioned that export controls could restrict shipments of advanced AI accelerators to certain international customers.
Production delays and supplier quality issues may defer aircraft deliveries and pressure free cash flow.
Management noted that changes in digital asset regulation could affect trading volumes and product availability.
Higher wage expense and transportation costs may pressure operating margin if pricing actions lag inflation.
Microsoft announced strong Azure growth and raised its capital expenditure outlook to support AI infrastructure demand.
Netflix added more subscribers than expected but warned that paid sharing benefits will moderate in future quarters.
Pfizer lowered full-year revenue guidance due to weaker demand for COVID-related products.
Meta authorized a larger buyback and reported advertising revenue growth above analyst expectations.
Management said it remains encouraged by AWS customer pipeline, while acknowledging enterprises are still optimizing cloud spend.
The CEO said the turnaround is taking longer than expected and near-term demand remains uncertain.
Management described cloud infrastructure demand as robust and said booked revenue backlog continues to expand.
Management emphasized cost discipline and improving streaming profitability, but noted linear TV weakness persists.
Shopify reported GMV growth of 22%, expanding free cash flow margin, and stronger merchant solutions revenue.
Snap reported slower advertising demand and said infrastructure costs will remain elevated.
Exxon generated higher upstream earnings due to stronger production volumes, partially offset by lower refining margins.
Salesforce reported operating margin expansion and raised full-year earnings guidance while revenue growth remained steady.
